generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11
recommended_mode_rationale: Drift in fast mode (0.27) passes ADR-004 strict, but argmax flips at position 2 (id 171 under fast vs id 863 under F64) — a near-tie that BF16 activation truncation tilts to a different token. For a chat model in production this is the structurally relevant failure mode: the next-token decision is wrong on 1 of 4 sampled positions. Certified mode is recommended; fast mode is acceptable only for workloads that explicitly tolerate occasional argmax flips (e.g. high-temperature sampling where the argmax at greedy is not the realised choice anyway).
per_tensor_policy_status: Reserved for M10.3+ dispatcher. Llama 3.2 uses the rope_scaling: llama3 piecewise frequency scaling (M4.6 Phase C); per-tensor analysis should pay particular attention to whether the RoPE-adjacent attention weights tolerate fast mode, since that's where the position-2 argmax flip is most likely originating.
